False zeal is that religious devotion and enthusiasm which is not based on truth. A zeal toward God which is "not according to knowledge," meaning the true knowledge of salvation, is false and unprofitable. (Rom. 10:2.) Before his conversion Paul manifested zeal "not according to knowledge" when he persecuted the Church. (Philip. 3:4-7.) (Bruce R. Mc Conkie, Mormon Doctrine, 2d ed., p.854)

They were a zealous and beloved people] Some people "have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge" (Romans 10:2). That is, their zeal-excitement, enthusiasm, eagerness-is not refined by the wisdom and judgment that must accompany righteous actions.
